zh.lifeSummary: 當Tibot or Mrs Iorwerth Ap Dafydd Llwyd於大約1330年出生在英國威爾斯卡迪甘史爾利安巴達 法福時，她的父親Ieuan ap Cadwgon432歲，母親Lleucu ferch Iorwerth427歲。她和Iorwerth ap David至少有3個兒子。她去世在威爾士凱爾納方史爾格林利方。

nameMeanings.name: Tibot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Catalan: nickname from a diminutive of Old French ribaut ‘man of bad live; vagabond’.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Mrs
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Iorwerth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: from iōr‘ ‘lord’ + a mutated form of berth ‘handsome’; borne in the Mabinogi by the jealous brother of Madawg , son of Maredudd . Iorwerth came to be regarded as a Welsh equivalent of Edward , but it has no actual connection with that name.

variants.name: Yorath
meanings: Anglicized form of   Iorwerth   or, in some cases, a transferred use of the surname derived from it.

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Tibot or Mrs Iorwerth Ap Dafydd Llwyd was born about 1330, in Llanbadarn fawr, Cardiganshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Ieuan ap Cadwgon, was 432 and her mother, Lleucu ferch Iorwerth, was 427. She had at least 3 sons with Iorwerth ap David. She died in Glynllifon, Caernarfonshire, Wales.

Elle a eu au moins 3 fils avec Iorwerth ap David. Elle est décédée à Glynllifon, Caernarfonshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Tibot or Mrs Iorwerth Ap Dafydd Llwyd um 1330 in Llanbadarn fawr, Cardiganshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ieuan ap Cadwgon 432 und ihre Mutter Lleucu ferch Iorwerth 427. Sie hatte mit Iorwerth ap David mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb in Glynllifon, Caernarfonshire, Wales.

ja.lifeSummary: Tibot or Mrs Iorwerth Ap Dafydd Llwydが約1330年にイギリスウェールズカーディガンシュアイアランバダルン ファウルで生まれた時，父Ieuan ap Cadwgonは432歳，母Lleucu ferch Iorwerthは427歳であった。彼女はIorwerth ap Davidとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼女はウェールズケエルナルフォンシュアイアグリンリフォンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Tibot or Mrs Iorwerth Ap Dafydd Llwyd(이)가 대략 1330년 영국 웨일스 카디건실르 랜바더른 파르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ieuan ap Cadwgon(은)는 432세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lleucu ferch Iorwerth(은)는 427세였다. 그녀는 Iorwerth ap David(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 웨일스 카이른아르폰실르 그린리폰에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Tibot or Mrs Iorwerth Ap Dafydd Llwyd родилась около 1330 г. в местности лланбадарн фар, кардиганьшир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Ieuan ap Cadwgon было 432 года, а матери, которую звали Lleucu ferch Iorwerth, 427. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына от Iorwerth ap David. Она умерла в местности глинллифон, каирнарфоншир, Уэльс.
